notes: Logical containment is normatively defined (rs-validation) over parsed, normalized URIs: same origin plus complete /-delimited path-segment-boundary ancestry — never raw string prefixing. "https://storage.example/alice" is a string prefix of "https://storage.example/aliceevil/…" but does not logically contain it.
source: lws-spec@deb310e core#client-rules (spec-derived; no reference implementation yet)
title: realm containment is logical (path-segment) containment, not naive string prefixing
